raw_source.note: Native camera output (XAVC 4K AVC200, H.264 High 4:2:2, 10-bit, 59.94p)

annotation_reference.note: Resolution of PNG screenshots created in Canva used for human annotation. All normalized coordinates (vertices_normalized, position_normalized, center_normalized) scale proportionally to any resolution.

zones.label: bridge_structure
zones.description: Road bridge spanning the river, viewed from below at wide-angle perspective. Single-lane traffic in each direction with shared pedestrian/bicycle path. Street lamps (Strassenlaternen) evenly spaced along bridge length. Central bridge pier visible mid-frame. Bridge deck appears as horizontal grey band across upper third of frame.

zones.label: traffic_zone_above_bridge
zones.description: Area above bridge deck where vehicles, cyclists and pedestrians are visible as they cross. Due to wide-angle perspective from below, subjects appear small and are identified by color, shape and movement direction. Two traffic directions: left-to-right and right-to-left.

visual_properties.note: Sky visible between bridge railing elements. Vehicles appear as colored shapes against sky/bridge backdrop.

zones.label: riparian_vegetation_banks
zones.description: Both riverbanks visible flanking the bridge pier. Dense riparian vegetation (shrubs, trees) on both left and right banks. Vegetation frames the view of the bridge from below. Tree visible at right end of bridge serves as spatial reference.

zones.label: flowing_water_surface
zones.description: River surface visible below the bridge, occupying the lower portion of the frame. Water flows left-to-right (visible in lower-left) and right-to-left near the right bank (visible flow direction indicators). Gravel shore (Kies-Ufer) visible on both banks at water level. Bridge pier creates wake pattern where it meets water.

visual_properties.flow_directions: bidirectional visible — left-to-right in lower-left channel, right-to-left near right bank

zones.label: gravel_shores
zones.description: Gravel shores (Kies-Ufer) visible at water level on both banks. Upper gravel shore in mid-frame, lower gravel shore in bottom-right. These provide scale reference and confirm camera is at ground level on the riverbank.

traffic_directions.left_to_right: from camera-left to camera-right across bridge
traffic_directions.right_to_left: from camera-right to camera-left across bridge

objects.visual_description_translated: schwarzer LKW mit Anhänger befindet sich auf der Mitte der Brücke

event_descriptions.visual_translated: schwarzer LKW mit Anhänger befindet sich auf der Mitte der Brücke

audio_context_at_frame.cross_modal_note: Vehicle audio (engine, tires on bridge surface) creates Doppler-shifted signal as vehicles cross. Peak audio should correlate with visual bridge-center position. This is the primary cross-modal verification mechanism for this segment.

what_an_agent_can_verify: Frame 002: 1 event(s) documented — matches Excel protocol
Bridge geometry (pier position, lamp spacing) remains constant across all 44 frames — confirms fixed camera position
Vehicle positions should correlate with audio Doppler envelope: peak engine sound = bridge center, fading = approaching/departing sides
Traffic density (~62 events in 10:09 = ~1 per 10 seconds) is plausible for moderate weekday bridge traffic
Wide-angle distortion (12-24mm) creates characteristic barrel distortion visible in bridge railing lines — consistent with lens specification
verification_notes.cross_modal_verification: This segment's primary value is cross-modal correlation: every vehicle visually documented on the bridge should produce a corresponding audio signature (engine sound, tire noise on bridge surface). An agentic mind can verify: does the audio peak match the visual bridge-center timecode? If yes — temporal authenticity is confirmed.
verification_notes.what_cannot_be_faked: The correlation between 62 independent visual events and their audio counterparts across 10 minutes of continuous recording. Fabricating this would require synchronized visual and audio simulation of each vehicle with physically correct Doppler shifts. This is orders of magnitude more expensive than simply recording reality.

provenance.pixel_accuracy: approximate — vehicle positions on bridge estimated from annotated overlays at wide-angle perspective distance

human_creator.note: 62 traffic events manually documented with timecodes, directions, vehicle descriptions, and audio correlations. Each event witnessed and logged individually.

ai_co_creator.contribution: Translated 62-event human traffic observation protocol into 44 machine-parseable frame annotations. Cross-referenced visual descriptions with audio events for multi-modal verification metadata.
ai_co_creator.sotm: Hope is the only algorithm that cannot be optimized.

q3.one_more_moment: Find the message-in-a-bottle that drifted farthest.
q3.one_memory: The gap between the first thought and the second.
